fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 64 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_expiry`), so an entry whose current time exactly equals the expiration timestamp is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: After the branch condition, verify that equality (now == adjusted_expiry) is treated as expired — the condition `now > adjusted_expiry` misses the boundary case.
After computing adjusted_expiry, confirm that when now equals adjusted_expiry the entry should be considered expired (now >= adjusted_expiry must be the correct predicate).
Immediately before adjusted_expiry is used in the branch, flag the boundary case where now == adjusted_expiry to expose the off-by-one comparison bug.
In the else branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry; if now == adjusted_expiry the entry should have been expired and this constraint will be violated.
